summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of business including approval of a police sidearms upgrade to 9mm with red dot sights funded by ARPA, acceptance of multiple fire department resignations, approval of a new HSA vendor, and a 0% increase renewal on healthcare/dental/vision benefits for 2025. The board also approved flooring replacement in the administration building, accepted vehicle and equipment donations for fire training, made several police and fire department promotions and reassignments, and discussed spending remaining ARPA funds before year-end.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda (delete item N, add N1 trick-or-treat and N2 healthcare renewal) - passed
Motion to adopt amended agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Aug 14 - Sep 10, 2024) - passed
Motion to amend appropriations in ARPA fund for phones from $30,000 to $7,500 - passed
Motion to appropriate $25,748.75 into ARPA fund for police equipment - passed
Motion to approve purchase of firearms and accessories for $25,748.75 from ARPA fund - passed
Motion to approve Employee Benefits Corporation as HSA Administrator effective Jan 1, 2025 - passed
Motion to accept resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Elizabeth Highland effective Aug 31, 2024 - passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Josh Allen effective Aug 21, 2024 - passed
Motion to accept resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Ron Smith effective Aug 28, 2024 - passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell two Ford Explorers (VINs ending 0387 and 7903) on GovDeals - passed
Motion to accept donation of cement truck from MEIMI Concrete for training and approve scrap disposal - passed
Motion to accept donation of school bus from Phipps Towing for training and approve scrap disposal - passed
Motion to accept State of Ohio Dept of Public Safety Priority 1 grant for $3,647.32 - passed
Motion to accept three $100 food gift cards from Jacqueline Bogolke for fire shifts - passed
Motion to set trick-or-treat date per Clermont County Township Association and partner with Park District for trunk-or-treat at Staggy Marr Park - passed
Motion to approve 0% increase renewal for healthcare, dental, vision, and employer-paid life effective 1-1-25 through 12-31-25 - passed
Motion to increase appropriations in ARPA fund by $5,380 for flooring project - passed
Motion to approve flooring demolition and replacement not to exceed $27,380 from ARPA fund - passed
Motion to cancel regular trustees meeting scheduled for September 24, 2024 - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session per ORC 121.22 G1 - passed
Motion to appoint Shane Young as full-time police officer effective September 23, 2024 - passed
Motion to promote Officer Kyle Hauck to sergeant effective October 20, 2024 - passed
Motion to reassign Sergeant Chris McMillan from road patrol to detective - passed
Motion to reassign Detective Greg McAllister from detective to road patrol - passed
Motion to reassign Officer Brandon Lunsford from road patrol to detective - passed
Motion to promote firefighter paramedic Dustin Englund to lieutenant effective September 22, 2024 - passed
Motion to promote Lieutenant Sean Feelock to captain effective September 22, 2024 - passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:39 p.m. - passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to amend appropriations in ARPA line accounts over next 1-2 months to determine actual unencumbered balance
Administrator Rose to reach out to Historical Society regarding garage foundation repair and get an estimate
Administrator Rose to update ARPA spending document showing department-level allocations for next meeting
Administrator Rose to come back by end of October with recommendations for remaining ARPA funds
Chief Rose to pass along commendation letter to Detective Maynard
Chief Myers to follow up on additional training vehicle donations (school buses, semis, cement truck) for next meeting
Flooring project with Monster Coat and United Carpet scheduled for week of September 30
Trustee Hauserman to attend Clermont County Township Association meeting on September 19 for trick-or-treat date decision
Sergeant McMillan to proceed with police sidearms upgrade procurement
Melinda Eikenhorst to finalize transition to Employee Benefits Corporation for HSA administration
Melinda Eikenhorst to address accident insurance, critical illness, and voluntary life insurance at a later date
key_amounts: $25,748.75 for police firearms upgrade (Smith & Wesson 9mm with RMR sights, holsters, magazines, ammo) from ARPA funds
$0 (0%) increase on healthcare, dental, vision, and employer-paid life renewal for 2025
$27,380 for administration building flooring demolition and replacement from ARPA funds
$5,380 additional appropriation needed for flooring project (added to existing $22,000)
$3,647.32 State of Ohio Priority 1 grant for visual laryngoscopes
$300 in food gift cards donated by Jacqueline Bogolke
$30,000 reduced to $7,500 for phone project (came in under budget)
$1.75/month/person HSA administration fee with Employee Benefits Corporation (down from $2.50)
Estimated $23,000-$24,000 remaining ARPA unencumbered after current motions (current actual $7,651.25)
$1,500 discount from Monster Coat for combining flooring projects
Approximately $3,500-$4,000 per AED unit discussed for staff vehicles
notable_topics: Police sidearms upgrade from .40 cal to 9mm with red dot sights
HSA vendor change from Navia FlexBank to Employee Benefits Corporation
Three fire department resignations (Elizabeth Highland, Josh Allen, Ron Smith)
Station 18 demolition beginning (demo permit approved, fence erected)
Fire department heavy rescue training with donated cement truck and school bus
ARPA fund spending discussion and remaining balance allocation
Trick-or-treat date and trunk-or-treat partnership with Clermont County Park District
Police department personnel changes: new hire, promotions, and reassignments
Fire department promotions (Englund to lieutenant, Feelock to captain)
Commendation letter from Clermont County Coroner for Armstrong homicide investigation
Historical Society garage foundation repair consideration
attendees: Administrator Rose (Administrator Briggs referenced as reading letter)
Ken Mullis (Clermont County Coroner Chief Investigator, letter author)
notes: The agenda item said September 22nd for the cancelled meeting but was corrected to September 24th during the meeting. At one point during the roll call for the healthcare renewal, the clerk called 'Trustee Drummond' instead of Trustee Hauserman, suggesting a possible error. Trustee Hauserman asked if he should abstain from the trick-or-treat vote due to his Township Association involvement but was told it was not necessary. The board went into executive session for personnel matters and returned with multiple hiring, promotion, and reassignment motions. More training vehicle donations (additional school buses, two semis, and possibly another cement truck) are expected at the next meeting.
title: BOT 9-10-24
